expectations.text: Response identifies the oversized CLAUDE.md as a problem and recommends a size limit
expectations.evidence: Lines 12-19: 'When CLAUDE.md exceeds one screen (roughly 800 tokens), it stops being a routing table and becomes a manual.' Three specific consequences listed.

expectations.text: Response identifies mixing reference docs with draft outputs as a distinct problem
expectations.evidence: Lines 21-35: Explicit anti-pattern with example tree showing 'output - WRONG LOCATION' labels. 'Claude cannot reliably distinguish: this is how I should always write (factory) from this is what I produced last run (product)'

expectations.text: Response explains WHY these problems cause context loss
expectations.evidence: Lines 14-19: Token bloat, context loss after message 2-3, instructions competing with artifacts. Line 35: 'model has internalized the wrong set of constraints from prior outputs.'

expectations.text: Response provides a concrete restructuring plan with specific directory structure
expectations.evidence: Lines 66-99: Before/after directory trees. Eight-phase implementation plan with specific file placement for each layer.

expectations.text: Response introduces the concept of separating stable references from working artifacts
expectations.evidence: Lines 52-63: Full Layer 3 vs Layer 4 table with 'the factory' and 'the product' labels. Token budget per layer. 'By separating them completely, Claude no longer has to guess which files are canonical.'
